changes.before: The child was maybe seven, pink-cheeked in the wrong way, her mother's hand fixed on her shoulder to keep her steady.
changes.after: The child was maybe seven, pink-cheeked in the wrong way, her mother holding her upright by the shoulder.
The captain set his mouth, and she let the silence do the rest.

changes.before: He heard the trap in that at once.

changes.after: It gets into the gut and keeps moving.

changes.before: He had heard some softened version of it; she let him sit with the fact that hers would be worse.
changes.after: The captain set his mouth, and she let the silence do the rest.

changes.before: Her read is worth the next few hours of this village.
changes.after: She can tell you what is poisoning them before nightfall.

changes.before: Water would have struck harder and stranger, day to day. This was steadier. Repeated meals. Repeated doses. Bread.
changes.after: Water would have struck harder and stranger, day to day, but this was steadier: repeated meals, repeated doses, bread.

changes.before: Instead she heard coughing somewhere behind the row of houses.
changes.after: Instead, coughing carried from behind the row of houses.

changes.before: He stared at the sacks as if they had become a different species of object in front of him.
Jaina understood only half of that and hated the half she understood.
A watcher, not the hand that built this.
changes.after: A watcher, not the one who tainted the grain.
